protected_files.reason: CI/CD pipeline definitions — incorrect edits can break releases, test runs, or the Docker image publish workflow.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Discuss pipeline changes with the team and test in a feature branch before merging to main.

protected_files.reason: Release bundling script that packages ultrainit.sh into the distributable single-file artifact. Errors here silently ship broken releases.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Run `make release` (or `make test-all` first) and verify the bundle output manually before pushing a tag.

protected_files.reason: Defines the isolated bats-core test environment. Changes affect every CI test run and the published GHCR image.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Build and verify locally with `make test-image` before committing. Changes trigger an automatic image rebuild on push to main.

protected_files.reason: Orchestrates build, test, and release targets. Incorrect changes break the developer workflow and CI.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Edit targets carefully; run `make test-all` after any change to verify nothing regressed.

protected_files.reason: Claude Code settings including hook wiring. Hand-editing can desync hooks from their scripts.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Use `ultrainit.sh` to regenerate, or use the Claude Code settings UI. For hook changes, update the hook script and re-run merge logic.

protected_files.reason: Security-critical: handles dependency checking, Claude auth verification (`claude auth status`), budget arithmetic, and environment setup. Errors here can silently bypass auth or blow the budget.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Make changes in a branch, add a corresponding unit test in `tests/unit/config_budget.bats` or `tests/unit/config_workdir.bats`, and run `make test-unit` before merging.

protected_files.reason: Security-critical: controls budget enforcement (`--max-budget-usd`), cost tracking, API response validation, and agent spawning. A bug here can cause runaway API spend or accept malformed outputs.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Add a test in `tests/unit/agent_run.bats` or `tests/unit/agent_helpers.bats` covering the changed behavior. Run `make test-unit` before merging.

protected_files.reason: Enforces quality gates on all generated artifacts (CLAUDE.md length, generic-phrase detection, hook wiring). Weakening checks here silently degrades output quality.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Add regression tests in `tests/unit/validate_claude_md.bats` or `tests/unit/validate_hook.bats` before modifying validation rules.

protected_files.reason: Standalone validator that enforces skill frontmatter, file-reference minimums, and generic-phrase bans. Used in Phase 5 and tested independently.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Update `tests/scripts/validate_skill.bats` alongside any rule changes and run `make test-scripts` to verify.

protected_files.reason: Standalone validator for subagent frontmatter and tool scoping. Same risk as validate-skill.sh.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Update `tests/scripts/validate_subagent.bats` alongside any rule changes and run `make test-scripts` to verify.

protected_files.reason: JSON schemas define the contract between orchestrator and every agent. Changing a schema breaks structured output parsing for all agents that use it, potentially silently producing empty findings.
protected_files.safe_alternative: When adding fields, add them as optional. Update the corresponding fixture in `tests/fixtures/findings/` and run `make test-all` to catch breakage.

protected_files.reason: Realistic fixtures that all unit and integration tests depend on. Incorrect edits cause tests to pass against a fantasy that doesn't match real claude output.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Capture real `claude -p --output-format json` output to update fixtures. Never hand-craft values — they must match the actual response envelope format.

protected_files.reason: Controls what gets committed. Accidentally removing `.ultrainit/` would commit intermediate findings (including potentially sensitive developer answers) to the repo.
protected_files.safe_alternative: Append new patterns with `echo 'pattern' >> .gitignore` rather than rewriting the file.
